---
title: Squared coefficient of variation
jupyter: python3
---

### Set up and load data

```{python}

## Import modules
import abct
import numpy as np
import plotly.express as px
from scipy import io

## Load data
data = io.loadmat("./data/hcp_data.mat")

```

### Set up data and functions

```{python}

# Get data
Wn = abct.kneighbor(data["W"], "common", kappa=0.1).toarray()
Cn = abct.kneighbor(data["X"].T @ data["X"], "common", kappa=0.1).toarray()

# Get squared coefficient of variation
WV = abct.dispersion(Wn, "coefvar2")
CV = abct.dispersion(Cn, "coefvar2")

# Get participation coefficients
K = np.arange(5, 30, 5)    # number of clusters
repl = 10                  # number of replicates

### Run Loyvain k-modularity
np.random.seed(1)
WP = [None] * len(K)
CP = [None] * len(K)
for i, k in enumerate(K):
    print(f"Number of clusters: {k}")
    Mw = abct.loyvain(Wn, k, "kmodularity", replicates=repl)[0]
    Mc = abct.loyvain(Cn, k, "kmodularity", replicates=repl)[0]
    WP[i] = abct.dispersion(Wn, "kpartcoef", Mw)
    CP[i] = abct.dispersion(Cn, "kpartcoef", Mc)

```

### Scatter plots of CV2 and participation coefficient

```{python}

normalize = lambda x: (x - x.min()) / (x.max() - x.min())

fig = px.scatter()
for i in range(len(K)):
    fig.add_scatter(
        x = - np.log10(1 - WP[i]),
        y = normalize(- WV),
        name = f"k = {K[i]}",
        mode="markers",
    )
fig.update_layout(
    xaxis_title="log-rescaled k-participation coefficient",
    yaxis_title="rescaled (- (Coefficient of variation)^2)",
    title = f"Structural network: r ~ {-np.mean(np.corrcoef(WV, np.array(WP))[0][1:]):.3f}",
    title_x=0.5,
    width=600,
    height=600,
)
fig.show()

fig = px.scatter()
for i in range(len(K)):
    fig.add_scatter(
        x = - np.log10(1 - CP[i]),
        y = normalize(- CV),
        name = f"k = {K[i]}",
        mode="markers",
    )
fig.update_layout(
    xaxis_title="log-rescaled k-participation coefficient",
    yaxis_title="rescaled (- (Coefficient of variation)^2)",
    title = f"Correlation network: r ~ {-np.mean(np.corrcoef(CV, np.array(CP))[0][1:]):.3f}",
    title_x=0.5,
    width=600,
    height=600,
)
fig.show()

```
